enonce.texte: Exercise 1: Match the banking terms with their correct definitions.

questions.texte:
Terms: 1. Account, 2. Deposit, 3. Withdraw, 4. Loan, 5. Interest

Definitions:

A. Money that a bank lends to a customer.

B. To take money out of a bank account.

C. An arrangement with a bank to keep money there.

D. The amount of money charged by a bank for borrowing money.

E. To put money into a bank account.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box.

Words: credit, debit, ATM, interest, deposit, withdraw, loan

questions.texte: 1. If you want to take money out of your account, you need to _______________ it.
2. You can use a _______________ card to pay for goods directly from your bank account.
3. A _______________ card allows you to borrow money to pay for things.
4. The bank charges _______________ when you borrow money from them.
5. To put money into your account, you need to make a _______________.
6. A cash machine is also called an _______________.
7. A _______________ is money you borrow from a bank and must pay back with interest.

intitule: Banking in Practice: A Day at the Bank

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

Dialogue:

Customer: Good morning. I would like to open a new bank account.

Bank Teller: Good morning. Sure. What type of account are you interested in?

Customer: A savings account, please. I also want to deposit some money today.

Bank Teller: Alright. You can deposit money at the counter or use the ATM. You can also withdraw money anytime.

Customer: Do you offer loans?

Bank Teller: Yes, we do. The interest rate depends on the amount and the duration of the loan.

Customer: Thank you. I will think about it.

questions.texte: What does the customer want to do?
questions.reponse: The customer wants to open a new savings account and deposit some money.

questions.texte: What are the ways to deposit money mentioned by the bank teller?
questions.reponse: The bank teller says the customer can deposit money at the counter or use the ATM.

questions.texte: According to the bank teller, what does the interest rate depend on?
questions.reponse: It depends on the amount of the loan and the duration of the loan.

questions.texte: Do you think it's a good idea to open a savings account? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Yes, I think it is a good idea because a savings account helps you to save money safely. It also allows you to earn interest on your savings and access banking services like loans and debit cards. It encourages financial discipline and helps you plan for the future.

intitule: Financial Decision Making: Choosing Between Debit and Credit

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Read the scenario and write a paragraph advising Mbot.

Scenario: Mbot is a 17-year-old student who has just received his first part-time salary. He wants to buy a new smartphone that costs 150,000 FCFA. He does not have enough money yet. He is considering using a credit card to pay for it or saving money for a few months to pay with cash. He has a bank account and a debit card.

questions.texte:
Write a paragraph advising Mbot. In your paragraph, you should:

  • Explain the difference between a debit card and a credit card.
  • Give at least two advantages and two disadvantages of using a credit card.
  • Tell Mbot what you think he should do and justify your advice.
questions.reponse: Dear Mbot, I understand you want to buy a new smartphone, but I advise you to think carefully before using a credit card. A debit card allows you to spend only the money you have in your account, while a credit card allows you to borrow money from the bank up to a certain limit. Using a credit card has advantages: you can get the phone immediately and you can build a good credit history. However, there are also disadvantages: you will have to pay interest on the money you borrow, and if you don't pay on time, you may have financial problems. In your case, I think you should save your salary for a few months and pay with cash or your debit card. This way, you will avoid debt and interest payments. It will also teach you the value of saving and patience. I hope this advice helps you make a wise decision.

intitule: Banking in Cameroon: A Comparative Analysis

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Read the following passage and answer the questions.

Banking in Cameroon

In Cameroon, banking services are available in both urban and rural areas. The Central Bank regulates all financial institutions. Many people use mobile money services like Orange Money and MTN Mobile Money because they are easy and accessible. Traditional banks offer services like savings accounts, loans, and money transfers. However, many people still prefer informal saving methods because they do not trust banks or find the fees too high. The government is working to improve financial inclusion by encouraging people to open bank accounts and use digital payment systems.

questions.texte: What is the role of the Central Bank in Cameroon?
questions.reponse: The Central Bank regulates all financial institutions in Cameroon.

questions.texte: List two mobile money services mentioned in the text.
Why do some people prefer informal saving methods?
questions.reponse: Some people prefer informal saving methods because they do not trust banks or find the fees too high.

questions.texte: What is the government doing to improve financial inclusion?
questions.reponse: The government is encouraging people to open bank accounts and use digital payment systems to improve financial inclusion.

questions.texte: Do you think mobile money is a good alternative to traditional banking? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Yes, I think mobile money is a good alternative because it is accessible, especially in rural areas where banks are not available. It is also easy to use with a basic phone and does not require high fees. Additionally, mobile money allows people to send and receive money quickly. However, it has limitations, such as transaction limits and lack of interest on savings. Traditional banks are better for long-term savings and large loans. Therefore, both systems have their place depending on the needs of the user.
